hypothesis: Fine-tuning a domain-specific language model on the MatPROV PROV-JSONLD graphs—using synthesis procedure graphs as training examples paired with their source text—will achieve higher structural-level F1-scores than the best-performing prompted general-purpose LLM (o4-mini with one-shot prompting, F1 ≈ 0.835) specifically for complex multi-branch synthesis procedures (those with >15 nodes), because fine-tuning will internalize the PROV-DM graph topology constraints and materials-domain terminology in a way that in-context prompting cannot, and this improvement will be measurable by comparing structural F1-scores stratified by graph node count across both approaches on a held-out test set annotated by multiple domain experts.
